newsArray.title: RCB get government nod, set to host IPL 2026 matches at M. Chinnaswamy
newsArray.description: M. Chinnaswamy Stadium has received formal approval from the Karnataka government to host Indian Premier League 2026 matches. This decision follows an expert review of safety measures after a recent stampede. Defending champions Royal Challengers Bengaluru will play their opening match and five home games at the venue.
creator: willow sports

newsArray.title: New Zealand to tour Bangladesh during IPL 2026, schedule announced for 3 ODIs and 3 T20Is
newsArray.description: Bangladesh Cricket Board (BCB) has confirmed the schedule for the national team's next series against New Zealand. The two teams will face each other in six white-ball matches - 3 ODIs and 3 T20Is - which will directly clash with the upcoming edition of the Indian Premier League (IPL) and Pakistan Super League (PSL).
creator: india tv news

newsArray.title: Was Gautam Gambhir behind Test retirements of Virat Kohli and Rohit Sharma?
newsArray.description: India head coach Gautam Gambhir has acknowledged that he may not have managed his relationship with senior cricketers Virat Kohli and Rohit Sharma perfectly during his tenure so far.Both Kohli and Rohit stepped away from Test cricket during Gambhir’s first season as head coach, following India’s disappointing performance in the Border-Gavaskar Trophy. Their retirements came amid speculation that tensions had developed within the dressing room.In a recent interaction, Gambhir was asked about reports suggesting his equation with the two veteran players had deteriorated. While he avoided commenting directly on the speculation, he admitted that mistakes might have been made during his time as coach.Gambhir stressed that errors are part of being human and said he is willing to accept them as long as his intentions remain honest.“I am human and I should be allowed to make mistakes. Players should also be allowed to make mistakes. Over the last 18 months I must have made some mistakes, and I don’t hesitate to admit that. But I believe one thing - a wrong decision taken with the right intent is acceptable. However, a wrong decision taken with the wrong intent has no place in the dressing room," said Gambhir.The former opener also emphasised the importance of transparency with players.“As long as I can be honest with my dressing room and speak to them by looking them in the eye, I believe I am doing a fair job,” he added.India’s struggles in Test cricket during series against New Zealand national cricket team and Australia national cricket team led to a review by the Board of Control for Cricket in India (BCCI). The board reportedly questioned Gambhir and then-captain Rohit Sharma over the team’s performance and later introduced stricter guidelines that limited certain freedoms previously enjoyed by players.Soon after the review, Kohli and Rohit - two of the most experienced members of the squad - announced their retirement from Test cricket.Also Read: Gambhir urges Dhoni to be India coach one day after warm World Cup exchangeSince then, the team has entered a transition phase under the leadership of Shubman Gill. Under Gambhir and Gill, India managed a draw in England, secured a series win against the West Indies national cricket team, but later suffered a setback in a two-Test series against the South Africa national cricket team.Although results have been inconsistent, the team management believes the new-look side is gradually shaping its identity in Test cricket.Meanwhile, Kohli and Rohit now represent India only in the ODI format and are aiming to feature in the 2027 Cricket World Cup. However, Gambhir and the selection panel have made it clear that even the two senior stars will have to earn their place in the squad rather than being automatic selections.
creator: rajendra mohapatra
